master_row.title: Universality of Elsasser Criticality
master_row.canonical_statement: The same critical behaviour emerges in small experiments and large astrophysical objects, reflecting a scaling functor that maps between these systems.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation relating ρ, μ, Ω, B.
master_row.dictionary: Across laboratory and astrophysical systems, the Elsasser critical parameter () reaches a universal value (_crit1) regardless of scale.
master_row.encyclopedia: The critical Elsasser value marks a universal balance architecture across scales: the point where magnetic organization and rotational inertia enter comparable strength. Interpretive role: This entry upgrades the Elsasser attractor from a local criterion into a universal comparative law. It underwrites later helicity, magnetic-moment, and scaling entries by establishing that order-one criticality persists across enormous changes of scale.
